grammarRule: After verb (and object), before adjectives/adverbs/prepositional phrases
grammarRuleDetail: nicht negates verbs and predicative adjectives. It comes after the verb and its direct object, but before any adjective, adverb, or prepositional phrase. Use kein- to negate nouns with indefinite articles.

english: yes (contradicting negation) / after all / come on
grammarRule: Unstressed: adds tone/nuance mid-sentence. Stressed: contradicts a negative statement.
grammarRuleDetail: doch has two main uses: (1) stressed — contradicts a negative (Nein! → Doch!), (2) unstressed modal particle — adds nuance of 'surely', 'after all', or slight impatience.
structureFormula: Doch! (stressed) | Das ist doch falsch. (unstressed particle)

usage.de: – Hast du kein Geld? – Doch, ich habe Geld.
usage.en: – Don't you have money? – Yes I do (contradiction).

grammarRule: Unstressed modal particle — softens requests and imperatives
grammarRuleDetail: mal is a colloquial modal particle derived from einmal (once). It softens imperatives and requests, making them sound more casual and friendly. It does not carry strong meaning on its own.
structureFormula: V + mal (softened imperative) | Kannst du mal …

usage.en: Could you help me for a second?

english: yes / as you know / after all (particle)
grammarRule: As answer: stressed = yes. As modal particle: unstressed = reminds listener of shared knowledge
grammarRuleDetail: ja has two uses: (1) as a simple answer 'yes', (2) as an unstressed modal particle meaning 'as you know' or 'after all' — indicating shared knowledge between speaker and listener.

usage.de: Du weißt ja, dass ich keine Zeit habe.
usage.en: You know (of course) that I have no time. (particle)

grammarRule: As adverb: sentence-level meaning of 'in principle / originally'. As particle: softens or redirects
grammarRuleDetail: eigentlich can be an adverb meaning 'actually / in principle' or an unstressed modal particle that softens a statement, implies a correction, or introduces a topic.
structureFormula: Eigentlich wollte ich … | Was machst du eigentlich?

usage.en: What do you actually do for a living? (curious)

usage.de: Eigentlich wollte ich heute früh schlafen.
usage.en: I actually wanted to go to bed early today.

grammarRule: Unstressed modal particle — expresses resigned acceptance, unchangeable facts
grammarRuleDetail: halt (more common in southern Germany and Austria; eben in northern Germany) signals that something is simply the way it is — the speaker accepts it as unchangeable. Very colloquial.
structureFormula: Das ist halt so. | Du musst halt warten.

variants.label: Combined
variants.form: Das ist halt nun mal so.

usage.en: That's just the way it is. (resigned)
You simply have to get up earlier.
